cookieConsent.title: Cookie notice
cookieConsent.description: Market Twin uses essential cookies to run the service and analytics cookies to improve usability.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Mr.AI Inc. (the 'Company') operates Market Twin (the 'Service') and processes user personal information in accordance with Korea's Personal Information Protection Act ('PIPA'). EU residents additionally retain rights under the GDPR. This policy describes how the Company collects, uses, shares, retains, and deletes personal information.

collect.title: Information We Collect
collect.body: We collect the following information to provide the service:
collect.item1: Account info: email address, password hash, login metadata (handled by Supabase Auth)
collect.item2: Workspace info: company name, industry, country — entered by you
collect.item3: Simulation inputs: product name, description, price, candidate countries, competitor URLs — entered in the wizard
collect.item4: Usage logs: page visits, simulation run history, IP address (operations and security purposes)

use.title: How We Use Information
use.body: Collected information is used solely for the following purposes:
use.item1: Running AI simulations and delivering results
use.item3: Email notifications about simulation outcomes (per user settings)
use.item4: Service stability / security monitoring and abuse prevention

share.title: Data Processors
share.body: We engage the following processors to operate the service. We do not sell or share user data for marketing or advertising purposes.
share.supabase: Database hosting and authentication (Seoul region — aws ap-northeast-2, data stored domestically in Korea)
share.vercel: Web application hosting (global edge CDN — see cross-border transfer section)
share.llm: AI simulation inference (simulation inputs are sent to LLM APIs — see cross-border transfer section)
share.resend: Transactional email delivery (see cross-border transfer section)

aiProcessing.title: AI Data Processing Notice
aiProcessing.body: The core feature of this service is simulation powered by Large Language Models (LLMs). Below is how the Company handles AI processing:
aiProcessing.input1: Simulation wizard inputs: product name, description, price, candidate countries, competitor URLs
aiProcessing.input2: Mr.AI chat messages and workspace memory (user-authored context)
aiProcessing.input3: Optional attachments (PDF, images) — only when explicitly uploaded by the user
aiProcessing.purpose1: Inputs received are used solely for Inference. They are NOT used for model Training.
aiProcessing.purpose2: Each LLM provider's enterprise API mode defaults to training opt-out, and the Company uses API accounts with training opt-out explicitly disabled (Anthropic Claude API, OpenAI API enterprise data policy, Google Vertex AI / AI Studio).
aiProcessing.provider5: Replicate — image generation models (only triggered explicitly by the user)
aiProcessing.optoutBody: AI inference is an essential feature of this service; refusing AI processing means you cannot use the service. If you do not consent to AI processing, please do not sign up; if you withdraw consent after signup, your account will be deleted by the Company.
aiProcessing.decisionBody: Simulation outputs (success score, recommended market, price, persona intent, etc.) are statistical estimates produced by LLM inference and are generated automatically without human review. Users may object to the result or request re-generation (re-run). If a result substantially influences a business decision, the user may request human review by contacting the Company.

crossBorder.title: Cross-Border Transfer of Personal Information (PIPA Art. 28)
crossBorder.body: The Company transfers user personal information and simulation inputs overseas as listed below. This is collected as a separate consent item; refusal means the service cannot be used.
crossBorder.row1Retention: Per Anthropic Data Usage Policy (0 days when ZDR option is enabled)
crossBorder.row2Retention: Per OpenAI Enterprise data policy (30-day abuse monitoring then deletion)
crossBorder.row3Retention: Per Google Cloud Data Processing Addendum
crossBorder.method: Method: API calls over TLS 1.2+ HTTPS encrypted channels. Transfers occur at the moment you run a simulation or compose a Mr.AI chat.
crossBorder.refusalNote: Right to refuse: Users may refuse cross-border transfer consent, but because the service depends on US-based LLM providers, refusal precludes account signup and simulation use.

retention.title: Retention
retention.body: We retain data until the user requests account deletion. Simulation results are retained indefinitely while a workspace is active and auto-deleted one year after a workspace is archived. Audit logs are retained for 5 years before anonymization.

rights.title: User Rights
rights.body: You have the following rights, exercisable at any time via the email at the end of this policy:

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.body: We use essential cookies for authentication sessions and analytics cookies to improve usability. On first visit you can decline analytics cookies via the consent banner. Essential cookies cannot be declined as they are required for the service to function.

changes.title: Changes to This Policy
changes.body: We may update this policy from time to time. For material changes we will notify users via email and in-service notice at least 7 days before the change takes effect; changes that are adverse to users or require new consent will require fresh consent.

dpo.title: Data Protection Officer (PIPA Art. 31)
dpo.body: The Company designates the following Data Protection Officer to oversee personal information processing and to handle inquiries, complaints, and remediation requests:
dpo.name: Name: Hyunwoo Lee
dpo.role: Title: CEO and Data Protection Officer
dpo.phoneNote: Phone inquiries: please request a call back via the email above.
dpo.remedyBody: Reports and consultations regarding privacy violations may be directed to: Personal Information Dispute Mediation Committee (+82-1833-6972, www.kopico.go.kr), Personal Information Infringement Report Center (+82-118, privacy.kisa.or.kr), Supreme Prosecutors' Office Cyber Investigation (+82-2-3480-3573), National Police Agency Cyber Bureau (+82-182).

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For general privacy questions please contact contact@markettwin.ai. For rights exercise and infringement reports please contact privacy@markettwin.ai.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These terms govern your use of the AI launch simulation service provided by Market Twin (the 'Company'). By signing up or using the service, you agree to these terms.

acceptance.title: Acceptance
acceptance.body: By creating an account or using the service, you agree to these Terms and the Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, you may not use the service.

service.title: Service Description
service.body: Market Twin provides AI-driven persona simulation tools that predict pre-launch market reaction. Using government statistics and market research, we deliver country-specific demand, pricing, competition, and persona-intent analyses.
service.disclaimer: Results are AI-derived estimates intended as reference material. They do not guarantee actual market outcomes. You must use results as guidance only; final decisions and accountability remain with you.

account.title: Account
account.body: You are responsible for providing accurate information and keeping your password secure. Notify us immediately if you discover unauthorized account use. Accounts follow a one-account-per-user principle; transfer or sharing may result in suspension.

acceptableUse.title: Acceptable Use
acceptableUse.item1: Violate laws, infringe others' rights, or simulate clearly illegal products / services
acceptableUse.item2: Conduct automated load attacks, reverse engineer, or access APIs without authorization
acceptableUse.item3: Access other users' data without authorization or attempt to bypass system security
acceptableUse.item4: Resell or commercially redistribute result data without our explicit written consent

ip.title: Intellectual Property
ip.body: All code, designs, database structure, and simulation algorithms are the property of the Company or its licensors.
ip.userContent: Product information you input belongs to you, and we process it only to provide the service. You may freely use simulation results generated by the service for your own business purposes.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.body: The service is provided 'AS IS', and we make no warranties as to the accuracy, completeness, or fitness for a particular purpose of results. AI model outputs are estimates and may differ from actual market outcomes.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, we are not liable for any direct, indirect, or incidental damages arising from use of the service. Our aggregate liability shall not exceed the amount you paid us during the prior 12 months.

termination.title: Termination
termination.body: You may stop using the service and request account deletion at any time. We may suspend or delete accounts for terms violations, with prior notice or immediately depending on severity.

law.title: Governing Law
law.body: These terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of Korea, with the Seoul Central District Court as the court of first instance. EU residents retain any additional rights granted by GDPR.

changes.title: Changes
changes.body: We may update these terms from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via email or in-service notice. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For questions about these terms, please contact hello@markettwin.app.

wizard.title: Create a new project

product.title: Product
product.description: Which product are we validating for overseas expansion? Tell us the name, category, and key description.

pricing.title: Pricing & Goal
pricing.description: Set the base price and launch objective. These directly drive persona evaluation and pricing recommendations.

countries.title: Target markets
countries.description: Which overseas markets are you expanding into? Gov-statistics-grounded AI personas are auto-generated per market.

competitors.title: Competitors
competitors.description: Reference competitor URLs. Optional — leave blank if you'd rather skip.

assets.title: Creative
assets.description: Test ad concepts before producing them. Describe each idea in plain text — AI personas evaluate fit per market and surface specific strengths and weaknesses, so you know which to greenlight.

review.title: Review & Run
review.description: Final check on the inputs, then start the simulation.

placeholders.description: e.g. Active noise-canceling wireless earbuds with 8h battery, IPX5 sweat resistance. Targeting commuters and remote workers. Available in matte black, sage, ivory.
placeholders.assetDescriptions:
Examples:
Hero video — morning routine concept
Instagram carousel — minimalist still life
3-min demo video — user testimonial interviews

hints.projectName: Internal identifier. Shown on the simulation result page.
hints.category: Category drives persona profession mix and channel recommendations. Pick the closest fit (cosmetics → Beauty, software tool → SaaS).
hints.description: Minimum 10 characters. Describe features, target audience, differentiation, ingredients, price tier. ⚠ Exclude external facts like market sales / share / current expansion / store count — the sim echoes those back in its recommendations and distorts accuracy (e.g., writing "US revenue 40%" auto-biases the sim toward US). Stick to the product itself for a more trustworthy market recommendation.
hints.basePrice: Current or candidate launch price. Simulation generates the curve at ±50% around this anchor.
hints.objective: Pick the #1 KPI for this launch. Persona scenarios and channel recommendations adjust accordingly.
hints.originatingCountry: Where the company is based and runs operations. Simulation evaluates candidate markets as cross-border export targets relative to this origin. Pick from 24 supported origins.
hints.countries: Pick at least 1 target market for overseas expansion. We recommend ≤5 to keep cost and runtime manageable.
hints.competitorNames: Type competitor product or brand names (one per line). The AI will find their URLs and suggest 2-3 more competitors you might not know about.
hints.competitorUrls: Optional — only if you already have specific URLs you want analysed. Otherwise leave blank; the AI finds URLs from the names above.
hints.assetDescriptions: What you'd run as ads (or are considering). One concept per line — rough descriptions are fine, finished visuals not required. Each gets a 0-100 fit score plus market-specific strengths and weaknesses in your results.
hints.assetUploads: Drop mockup images here if you have them. Vision-AI inspects each visual to sharpen the analysis. Either this or URLs below — pick whichever is easier; both work equally.
hints.assetUrls: Already-hosted CDN / Imgur / Drive URLs (one per line). Identical effect to uploading the file directly. Skip if you uploaded above. URLs must be publicly accessible.

descriptionStrength.title: Description quality
descriptionStrength.tipsHeader: Add these to improve sim accuracy:
descriptionStrength.strong: Concrete enough — sim can leverage your market signals well.

tips.length: Expand to 80+ characters (include features, differentiators, context).
tips.channel: Mention distribution channels (e.g. Amazon, TikTok Shop, Sephora, Olive Young, duty-free, D2C).
tips.demographic: Specify target demographic (e.g. 20-30s women, Gen Z, working moms, young professionals).
tips.audience: Add audience signals (e.g. Reddit r/AsianBeauty, Hyram followers, K-pop fans, influencer marketing).
tips.geography: Regional/cultural positioning (e.g. US market, EU, Southeast Asia, global, Western consumers).
tips.price_tier: Price tier (e.g. mass, mid-price, premium, luxury, $20-30, around ₩30,000).

brandStrategy.title: 🧭 Brand strategy hints (optional)
brandStrategy.intro: Brand-specific detail that macro anchors (population, income, tariff data) can't see. Even one or two lines help. Leaving blank is fine — sim still runs; this is an accuracy boost channel.

founder.label: Founder / core team background
founder.hint: Who is building this brand? Influencer-turned-founder, domain expert, overseas distribution veteran — backgrounds that change market-entry odds.
founder.placeholder: e.g. Influencer-founder, group-buy origin (2017) / 15-yr footwear OEM + in-house R&D / Ex-Japanese luxury distribution / US K-beauty reviewer community

channel.label: Channel priority
channel.hint: Where you'll sell first, if decided. Strong signal for "which market fits."
channel.retail_first: Retail-first (Sephora, Ulta, Olive Young Global, Watsons)

kol.label: KOL / influencer relationships
kol.hint: Existing / in-discussion influencer relationships, ambassador deals, or organic community followings — leading indicator of viral moments.
kol.placeholder: e.g. Founder's own IG 500k + 5 JP TikTok creators in negotiation / Reddit r/AsianBeauty organic mentions / Kendall Jenner endorsement Q3 2027

examples.title: 📚 See examples — how detailed should this be?
examples.intro: Three typical archetypes showing the kind of detail that helps the sim. If one resembles your case, click "Use this example" to prefill — then edit to fit your brand.
examples.applied: ✓ Applied — edit to fit your case

kolNative.name: KOL-native D2C (founder-as-influencer)
kolNative.founder: Influencer-founder started as group-buy in 2017, incorporated 2019. No traditional ATL ad budget. D2C site + domestic Olive Young as anchor channels.
kolNative.kol: Founder's own IG 500k + 5 JP TikTok cosmetic creators in negotiation. US market: Reddit r/AsianBeauty organic mentions starting to surface.

domainExpert.name: Domain-expert founder (ex-manufacturer)
domainExpert.founder: 15 years footwear OEM + proprietary merino wool R&D. 3 years running domestic D2C, ~₩200M/month revenue. Now preparing overseas launch.
domainExpert.kol: 3 Korean clean-celebrity lifestyle influencers under collaboration (vs Allbirds positioning). JP KOL strategy undecided.

indieOrganic.name: Indie + Western organic following
indieOrganic.founder: Started 2016 as solo indie, acquired by US D2C distributor in 2019. ~₩100M annual revenue. Minimal Korea domestic marketing.
indieOrganic.kol: Organic Reddit r/AsianBeauty following + Western K-beauty YouTubers (James Welsh era) covering the brand unsolicited. No paid KOL contracts.

validation.description: Description must be at least 10 characters

objectiveHint.awareness: Focus on new-brand exposure and content reach
objectiveHint.conversion: Drive immediate revenue at launch — pricing-optimized
objectiveHint.retention: Strengthen repeat purchase and LTV — loyal customer focus
objectiveHint.expansion: Expanding from existing market to new countries

wizard.countriesOriginHint: 💡 Adding the origin market as a candidate computes a domestic score (for comparison). Note: the recommended target (Top-2) is selected from the EXPORT candidates only — domestic is a baseline, not a recommendation. To do a deep-dive on the home market alone, select just the origin (1 candidate).
wizard.competitorResolverHint: 💡 The AI resolves URLs for the names you typed and adds 2-3 more relevant competitors. Adds ~3-8 seconds to project creation. Results are split into "your input" and "AI-discovered" so you always see attribution.
wizard.assetUrlsAccuracyHint: 💡 Text descriptions work great for pre-production validation. Uploads are optional — add them later when you have mockups for sharper visual analysis.
wizard.assetUploadsCta: Click to choose mockup images (or drag in)
wizard.assetUploadsLimits: JPG / PNG / WebP / GIF · up to 4 MB each
wizard.assetUrlsToggle: Already have hosted URLs? Paste them here

personaCount.label: Persona count
personaCount.hint: Higher count = more accurate, but takes longer.

tier.label: Analysis depth
tier.hint: Confidence scales with the number of personas and parallel sims. Every tier aggregates N independent runs and surfaces consensus + variance alongside the headline recommendation. Recommended workflow: start with Hypothesis to check Top-1 dominance vs Top-2 cluster, then commit to Consensus if a deeper narrative is needed.

notifyEmail.label: Email notification (optional)
notifyEmail.hint: Consensus and above take 10+ minutes. We'll email you the result link if you enter an address.

templates.title: Start from a template
templates.description: Pick a category preset — every field gets pre-filled. Edit freely after clicking.

simulation.feelPremium: AI personas are evaluating your product
simulation.cancelConfirm: Cancel this simulation? Analysis progress so far will not be saved.
simulation.cancelFailed: Couldn't cancel the simulation. Please try again in a moment.
simulation.pollWarning: Status updates are briefly delayed — auto-retrying

failed.title: Simulation failed
failed.subtitle: Something went wrong during analysis. Please try again.
failed.retryFailed: Couldn't start the retry. Please try again in a moment.

cancelled.title: Simulation cancelled
cancelled.subtitle: This simulation was cancelled by an operator.

help.successScore: Overall launch success probability (0-100). Combines average persona purchase intent, country score, and regulatory risk into a single number.
help.bestCountry: The candidate country with the highest combined score. AI recommends prioritizing marketing resources here first.
help.bestSegment: The persona segment in the top country with the highest purchase intent (profession / age / behavior summary).
help.bestPrice: AI's recommended entry price after analyzing the pricing curve and persona price sensitivity.
help.bestCreative: The highest-scoring creative asset (only shown when creative inputs were provided in the wizard).
help.riskLevel: Overall launch risk level (LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH). Combines regulatory, competitive, and supply-chain factors.

help.rank: Entry priority based on combined score and market attractiveness. Rank 1 is the most recommended market.
help.demand: Estimated market demand for this product category in this country (0-100). Reflects market size and purchase intent.
help.cac: Estimated customer acquisition cost (USD) per new customer. Reflects ad rates, channel efficiency, and competition intensity.
help.competition: Number and market-share distribution of existing competitors in the same category (0-100). Higher score = harder to enter.
help.score: Weighted final entry score across demand / CAC / competition (0-100). 100 = strongly recommended market.
help.rationale: AI's reasoning for the score. Click the row to expand into persona analysis and regulatory notes.

drill.noPersonas: No personas assigned to this country.

persona.anchoredHint: Persona generation referenced gov-statistics from the sources above.

help.intentLegend: Purchase intent (0-100) is the persona's likelihood of actually buying the product. 70+ is a strong purchase signal; below 35 is hypothetical or hesitant.

help.recommendedPrice: Optimal entry price computed from demand, persona price sensitivity, and competitor price tier. May not be the revenue-max point (margin / positioning weighted in).
help.marginEstimate: Margin commentary at the recommended price. Actual cost structure should be verified separately by the user.

why.title: Why this price
why.interpHigh: Price sensitivity skews high — lower price points convert better.
why.interpLow: Price sensitivity skews low — premium pricing is viable.
why.interpMixed: Price sensitivity is mixed — consider tiered pricing (good / better / best).
why.recommendedHint: At this price: {conv}% conversion, {rev} revenue index
why.peakHint: Revenue index of {rev} — highest point on the curve

recommendations.emptyActionPlan: No action plan was generated — try re-running the simulation.

results.pdfFailed: Couldn't generate the PDF. Please try again in a moment.

risks.linkedObjectionsHint: Persona objections sharing keywords with this risk
risks.noLinkedObjections: No persona objections directly match this risk.
risks.weakestMarketsHint: Lowest-scoring markets — exposure to this risk is likely highest here.
risks.mitigatingTrustFactorsHint: Trust factors valued by personas affected by this risk
risks.noMitigation: No trust signals registered from affected personas.
risks.anchoredHint: Personas were anchored on the gov-statistics sources above.

legend.title: Impact delta
legend.help: Delta = avg purchase intent of affected personas minus overall average. The more negative, the more this risk actually depresses conversion (rather than just being a hypothetical concern).

risks.rankHelp: Risks ranked by number of affected personas — #1 is the risk that touches the most personas in the simulation.

regulatory.title: Regulatory check
regulatory.excludedDesc: Auto-excluded from the simulation because this product is legally banned for sale in these markets
regulatory.restrictedDesc: Sale is permitted but with non-trivial constraints (license, prescription, age, regional)
regulatory.noIssues: This product can be legally sold in all candidate countries.

stageFailures.empty: No failures in the last 7 days.

providers.title: LLM Provider Health (7 days)

stuck.description: Jobs still in 'running' state past 6 minutes — zombies left over after the Vercel function was killed. Cancel or retry them.

tabs.settingsSubtitle: Onboarding · external integrations · image generation · presets.

chat.inputPlaceholder: Ask Mr. AI or share a fact. (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline)
chat.emptyHint: Start by telling Mr. AI about your company, product, and role. Anything important is auto-remembered and surfaces in future conversations.
chat.exampleA: "Our company is Acme — a SaaS for shoe brands tracking secondary market demand. ICP: indie footwear founders."
chat.exampleB: "Keep reports short, in English, lead with numbers."

memory.title: Persistent Memory
memory.empty: No memories saved yet. As you chat, Mr. AI will remember company/product/preferences automatically.
memory.deleteConfirm: Forget this memory? Mr. AI won't know it anymore.

briefing.title: Today's Briefing
briefing.empty: No briefing yet. Click "Generate now" to compose one from saved memories + recent conversations.

content.title: Content Briefs
content.subtitle: Enter a topic → ContentStrategist plans the angle using workspace memory + KG. Sprint 6 will draft the actual body.
content.empty: No briefs yet. Create one to run the ContentStrategist.

form.goalPlaceholder: e.g. SEO traffic · thought leadership · lead gen

channels.title: Auto-publish channels
channels.subtitle: Send briefings automatically to Slack, email, or webhooks
channels.empty: No channels yet. Add one and your briefing will publish automatically.
channels.slackHelp: Slack workspace settings → Apps → Incoming Webhooks → pick a channel → copy Webhook URL
channels.emailHelp: Requires RESEND_API_KEY env var to actually send.

integrations.disconnectConfirm: Disconnect {provider}? Synced signals will be removed.
integrations.envHint: Set HUBSPOT_CLIENT_ID, HUBSPOT_CLIENT_SECRET, and APP_BASE_URL in your environment.
